summary: The submitted article explains AI-assisted estimating but buries its core concepts below the fold and ships no machine-readable views, so an agent must read the whole page to learn what it teaches.

findings.title: No first-fetch answer
findings.detail: The opening 200 words do not state what the page teaches or who it serves, so a first-fetch agent cannot act without reading further.

findings.title: Thin entity coverage
findings.detail: Key entities such as job pricing and demand forecasting are named once and never linked to a defining page.

findings.title: Citations lack publishers
findings.detail: Two external claims cite a URL but not the publisher, weakening source trust signals.

materializedViewOpportunities: Publish a /article.md markdown twin advertised via rel=alternate
Emit JSON-LD for the core HowTo so agents can extract steps
priorityFixes: Add a first-fetch answer paragraph in the opening 150 words
Link the five core entities to their pillar pages with descriptive anchors
Ship a markdown twin and JSON-LD block
remediationPrompt: Rewrite the article so the first 150 words answer the core question, add internal links for the five named entities to their pillar pages, and emit a markdown twin plus a HowTo JSON-LD block. Keep all factual claims and add the publisher for each citation.
